phase: Phase P3A-RPC Production Mainnet RPC Readiness
summary: Prepared a server-side RPC strategy so P3A can use a production-grade mainnet RPC without exposing a paid api-key to the browser. The paid RPC belongs in a server-only Worker secret named SOLANA_RPC_URL (set via `wrangler secret put`, never committed to vars). Browser LIFE++ balance/eligibility reads are routed through a new production server route /api/solana/lifepp-balance, which resolves the secret via getCloudflareContext().env and reads the chain server-side; the client never receives the RPC URL. NEXT_PUBLIC_SOLANA_RPC_URL stays the public, keyless, rate-limited placeholder. No deploy, no workflow dispatch, no root-domain change, no transfer/burn/signing/canary/transaction/treasury mutation.
secretSetCommand: npx wrangler secret put SOLANA_RPC_URL --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc

priorityOrder: process.env.SOLANA_RPC_URL (server-only secret; never NEXT_PUBLIC; undefined in browser)
rpcResolution.browserBehavior: readLifePlusBalanceRaw is environment-aware: when typeof window !== 'undefined' it fetches /api/solana/lifepp-balance instead of opening a client-side RPC Connection.
rpcResolution.serverBehavior: readLifePlusBalanceForOwner(owner, rpcUrlOverride?) reads the chain directly; the production route injects the secret URL resolved via getCloudflareContext().env.

serverRoute.scope: reads only the LIFE++ associated-token-account balance for the supplied wallet; no sign, build, submit, or broadcast.

answers.isRpcClientExposedOrServerSide: Both paths exist. The PUBLIC placeholder (NEXT_PUBLIC_SOLANA_RPC_URL) remains client-visible by design but carries no key. The PRODUCTION/paid RPC is server-side only (SOLANA_RPC_URL secret) and is never inlined into client JS.

filesChanged: src/lib/lifePlusSolana.ts (resolveRpcUrl prefers SOLANA_RPC_URL; getConnection rpcUrlOverride; environment-aware readLifePlusBalanceRaw; new readLifePlusBalanceForOwner + readLifePlusBalanceViaServer + LIFEPP_BALANCE_API_PATH)
wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc (doc-only: public placeholder marked keyless; SOLANA_RPC_URL secret documented; not added to vars)
callSitesUnchanged: src/components/Gatekeeper.tsx (verifySolanaPocc still calls readLifePlusBalanceRaw(connection) — now auto-routed server-side in the browser)
knownNonBlocking: LifePaymentModule dry-run still builds a tx client-side against NEXT_PUBLIC_SOLANA_RPC_URL (public placeholder) to verify shape (getLatestBlockhash). It never sees the paid secret, never signs, and never broadcasts; in live-readonly the transfer is structurally impossible. Rate-limiting only affects the best-effort dry-run shape check.

The /api/solana/lifepp-balance route is an open readonly LIFE++ balance oracle (no auth, pre-gate). It only reads the LIFE++ ATA for a supplied wallet. Per-IP rate limiting to protect paid RPC quota is a follow-up before wide exposure.

validation.note: Real exit codes captured; see commit run. No tail-masking.

blockers.detail: Operator must set the server-only secret before a real P3A run: `npx wrangler secret put SOLANA_RPC_URL --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc` with a production RPC (Helius/Triton/QuickNode/Alchemy). Until then the server route falls back to the public, rate-limited endpoint.

This shell has no Cloudflare/wrangler auth and intermittent outbound HTTPS via the sandbox proxy. Deploy is an explicit operator action from an authenticated host (opennextjs-cloudflare build/deploy with --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc).

rollbackInstructions: No deploy occurred — nothing to roll back. Secret rotate/remove: `npx wrangler secret put|delete SOLANA_RPC_URL --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc`. If a P3A worker is later deployed and needs teardown: `npx wrangler delete --name ahin-io-p3a` (separate worker; never touches root ahin-io or ahin.io).
nextPhase: P3A deploy (operator action, requires SOLANA_RPC_URL secret + Cloudflare auth) -> clean readonly observation. P3B (single foundation-wallet micro-transfer) remains BLOCKED and additionally requires allowlist + cap + kill-switch drill + OOB mint/treasury/ATA verification + explicit operator authorization.
